payload.text:
Crie uma landing page completa e responsiva (mobile-first) em HTML + CSS + JS (ou no formato nativo do builder, se aplicável), com design premium, limpo e moderno, focado em conversão.
Idioma: Português (Brasil).

OBJETIVO:
Vender o “Pacote Bíblia em Foco – Materiais Visuais” por R$ 37,90 (pagamento único), com CTA para checkout.

REGRA PRINCIPAL:
NÃO EXISTE “PRODUTO PRINCIPAL”. 
O site deve apresentar TUDO como um único pacote/biblioteca visual. 
Nada de “Módulo principal”, “produto base”, “pilar 1/2”. 
Tudo é parte do mesmo pacote.

IDENTIDADE VISUAL:
- Estilo: editorial premium cristão (sofisticado, sem infantilizar).
- Paleta: dourado suave + azul profundo + bege/pergaminho (bem sutil).
- Tipografia: serif elegante nos títulos + sans clean no corpo.
- Layout: bastante respiro, cards, seções bem separadas, ícones minimalistas.
- Backgrounds: textura de pergaminho/mapas muito leve (quase imperceptível).

DADOS DA OFERTA:
Nome: Pacote Bíblia em Foco – Materiais Visuais
Preço: R$ 37,90 (pagamento único)
Acesso: vitalício + imediato
Garantia: 7 dias
Formato: materiais digitais (PDF) para celular, tablet e PC

PROMESSA CENTRAL (usar no topo e repetir ao longo):
“Estude a Bíblia com clareza visual do Gênesis ao Apocalipse: mapas, imagens e guias que organizam o contexto e tornam o entendimento simples.”

O QUE ESTÁ INCLUSO NO PACOTE (tratar como “Coleção Completa”):
1) Geografia Bíblica Ilustrada (mapas, cenários, rotas, contexto)
2) Apocalipse Revelado (símbolos, criaturas, selos, trombetas, juízo, Nova Jerusalém)
3) Cartas aos Apóstolos
4) 57 Respostas aos Cristãos
5) Mapas Mentais da Bíblia
6) Resumo Completo da Bíblia
Bônus visuais do pacote:
- Atlas Bíblico em Alta Resolução
- Cronologia Ilustrada dos Reis
- Guia de Pesos e Medidas Bíblicas
- Dicionário Geográfico Ilustrado
- Reconstrução 3D de Cidades
- Guia de Estudo para Pequenos Grupos

PÚBLICO:
Pastores, pregadores, professores de EBD, líderes de pequenos grupos, estudantes de teologia e leitores que querem profundidade sem confusão.

DORES (transformar em texto de identificação):
- “Eu leio, mas não consigo visualizar os cenários.”
- “Nomes e lugares parecem palavras soltas.”
- “Sem mapas, eu não entendo distâncias e contexto.”
- “Apocalipse confunde porque símbolos não são explicados de forma clara.”
- “Pra ensinar, falta material visual que segure o estudo.”

MENSAGEM-CHAVE (posicionamento):
Este pacote é uma biblioteca visual para quem quer:
- entender com clareza
- reter mais
- ensinar com segurança
- estudar sem ficar perdido

CAPAS (usar como imagens do site):
Crie uma seção “Coleção Incluída” com grade de 6 cards (capa + nome + 1 linha de benefício).
Use placeholders de imagem, serão substituídos depois:
/IMAGES/apocalipse.png
/IMAGES/geografia.png
/IMAGES/cartas.jpg
/IMAGES/57respostas.jpg
/IMAGES/mapas_mentais.jpg
/IMAGES/resumo.jpg
Se faltar alguma capa, use placeholder elegante com fundo pergaminho.

ESTRUTURA OBRIGATÓRIA DA PÁGINA (com copy pronta em cada seção):

1) HERO (primeira dobra)
- Headline forte (sem mencionar produto principal)
Sugestão de headline:
“Uma biblioteca visual completa para entender a Bíblia do Gênesis ao Apocalipse.”
- Subheadline:
“Mapas, imagens e guias práticos para estudar com clareza, reter mais e ensinar com segurança.”
- 3 bullets rápidos:
• Visualize cenários e rotas bíblicas
• Entenda símbolos e profecias sem confusão
• Tenha materiais de consulta rápida para qualquer estudo
- Preço destacado: R$ 37,90
- Badges: “Acesso Vitalício” + “Acesso Imediato” + “Garantia 7 dias”
- CTA principal: “Quero meu acesso ao Pacote”
- Microcopy: “Pagamento único. Sem mensalidades.”

2) SEÇÃO “POR QUE A MAIORIA TRAVA NO ESTUDO”
3 cards com dor:
- “Bíblia no modo texto” (não visualiza)
- “Contexto perdido” (distâncias, rotas, cenários)
- “Profecias confusas” (símbolos sem clareza)
Fechar com:
“A solução é estudar com suporte visual e organização — tudo reunido em um só pacote.”

3) SEÇÃO “O QUE VOCÊ CONQUISTA COM O PACOTE”
3 benefícios em cards:
- Clareza imediata (entendimento sem enrolação)
- Retenção maior (visual fixa)
- Segurança para ensinar (EBD, estudos, sermões)

4) SEÇÃO “O QUE TEM DENTRO DO PACOTE” (A COLEÇÃO COMPLETA)
Aqui deve ficar MUITO claro que é UM PACOTE.
- Grid com 6 itens (cada um com:
Nome + 1 frase de benefício + “Incluso no pacote”)
Itens:
Geografia Bíblica Ilustrada
Apocalipse Revelado
Cartas aos Apóstolos
57 Respostas aos Cristãos
Mapas Mentais da Bíblia
Resumo Completo da Bíblia

5) SEÇÃO “BÔNUS VISUAIS INCLUSOS”
Lista com ícones e frase curta em cada:
Atlas HD / Cronologia / Pesos e Medidas / Dicionário / Reconstruções 3D / Guia pequenos grupos
Texto de reforço:
“Tudo incluso no mesmo acesso. Nada separado.”

6) SEÇÃO “VEJA POR DENTRO”
Galeria de 8 placeholders de páginas internas:
/IMAGES/pagina1.jpg ... /IMAGES/pagina8.jpg
Texto curto:
“Layout editorial premium, leitura fácil e visual forte.”

7) SEÇÃO “PARA QUEM É”
Lista com ícones:
Pastores e pregadores
Professores de EBD
Líderes de pequenos grupos
Estudantes de teologia
Leitores que querem profundidade
+ Nota: “Se você quer clareza e contexto, é pra você.”

8) SEÇÃO “OFERTA”
- Mostrar preço R$ 37,90
- Reforçar: “Pagamento único • Acesso vitalício • Acesso imediato”
- CTA: “Desbloquear meu acesso agora”
- Selos: “Pagamento 100% seguro”

9) GARANTIA
Card:
“7 dias de garantia. Teste sem risco.”

10) FAQ (curto e objetivo)
- Como recebo o acesso?
- É impresso ou digital?
- Funciona no celular?
- Preciso de internet?
- Por quanto tempo tenho acesso?
- Como pedir reembolso?
- Como falar com suporte?

11) CTA FINAL + RODAPÉ
Headline:
“Comece hoje seu estudo com clareza visual.”
CTA:
“Quero meu acesso ao Pacote”
Rodapé:
© Bíblia em Foco – Materiais Visuais
Links: Termos / Privacidade / Suporte (placeholders)

EXTRAS DE CONVERSÃO:
- Barra fixa no mobile com botão “Quero acesso”
- Microcopy embaixo de CTA: “Acesso liberado logo após a confirmação do pagamento.”
- Animações leves (fade/slide) opcionais, sem pesar.
- Não usar depoimentos falsos como reais.

ENTREGUE O RESULTADO:
Código completo do site (HTML + CSS + JS) pronto para publicar.

**Crafting a Landing Page**

I'm now focused on the initial concept for the landing page. It's becoming clear that a mobile-first, responsive design is essential for the "Pacote Bíblia em Foco" visuals. The target is a premium Christian aesthetic that's sophisticated, clean, and modern, and the package will be priced at R$ 37,90.


**Developing the Visual Framework**

I'm solidifying the color palette and typography choices. Soft gold, deep blue, and a subtle beige are coming together nicely. I'm leaning toward a serif for elegant titles and a sans-serif for body text, maintaining a spacious, card-based layout. The content structure is taking shape, incorporating sections for pain points, benefits, the package's contents, visual bonuses, and an inside-look gallery. I've chosen React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Lucide React, and Framer Motion as key technologies. The focus is on a single, compelling bundle.


**Constructing Component Library**

My attention is now on the implementation details. I'm building out the component structure, starting with the base `index.html` and entry point `index.tsx`. I'm using Google Fonts (Cinzel for headings, Inter for body). I'm structuring `App.tsx` for section placement. I'm building reusable components for Header, Hero, PainPoints, Benefits, Collection, VisualBonuses, InsideLook, TargetAudience, Offer, Warranty, FAQ, and Footer, as well as a StickyCTA for mobile.


**Defining Visual Extras**

I'm now refining the content elements to adhere to the "single package" focus. "Bonuses" are being reframed as "Visual Extras". I've finalized the structure, and I've integrated responsive design with Tailwind's prefixes. The Hero section is now defined, outlining the headline, subheadline, bullets, price, and badge. Pain Points, Benefits, and the Collection Grid are also ready, with 8 placeholders in "Inside Look". FAQ items are defined, and the section reveal motion and sticky CTA are implemented. I'm prioritizing accessibility.

payload.text: quero que deixe a fonte do site mais elegante e bonita, o site precisa de sombreamente e mais contrastes

**Considering Refinements**

I'm currently focused on the landing page's aesthetic and aim to make it more premium. My immediate task is finding elegant and beautiful font combinations. I'm prioritizing typography that conveys quality and sophistication.


**Revising Visual Depth**

I'm now diving deeper into how to elevate the page's visual depth. I'm focusing on the elegant text display and plan to introduce more sophisticated shadows and improve the contrast to highlight key elements and enhance readability. I'll need to explore some custom shadow utilities and also update the `App.tsx` file for a more polished look.


**Enhancing Text Display**

I'm now revising my text display approach. I've decided to pair 'Cinzel' and 'Playfair Display' for a sophisticated, magazine-like header look, and I'll keep 'Inter' for the body, but with varied weights and letter-spacing for improved readability and visual interest. I'm planning to introduce subtle text shadows to the large headings and implement a more dramatic gradient overlay for the hero section to improve the overall aesthetic. I will also increase padding throughout the sections and deepen the visual style of the cards, so that the elements on the page look more visually appealing.


**Improving Visual Elements**

I'm now focusing on specific visual refinements. I've decided to incorporate subtle glow effects on button hovers and implement ornate borders, perhaps using double lines or thin gold accents. I'm exploring an aged-paper effect using a radial gradient and I'm refining the shadows to create a floating appearance using a specific `box-shadow` style. Additionally, I will refine the color contrasts, particularly ensuring a deeper blue and a richer gold, and start working on the XML.

entries.description: Update typography with more elegant fonts (Playfair Display and Cormorant Garamond) and refine global styles for better depth and contrast.
Enhance visual hierarchy, add elegant font pairings, improve shadows, and increase overall contrast for a more premium editorial feel.

payload.text: troque a fonte e diminua a sombra, deixe uma pagina mais leve

**Adjusting Visual Weight**

I'm currently focused on the visual impact. The deep blue background and heavy shadows are established, and I'm now exploring how to lighten the overall feel as requested. Changing the font will be next, then adjusting the shadows to be lighter. The page weight is my current focus.


**Refining the Aesthetic**

I'm now prioritizing the visual lightness, having already established a foundation. The fonts are up next; I'm leaning toward Outfit for headings and Lora for body text, intending a balance between modern and elegant. Shadow intensity is being dialed back significantly, aiming for subtle elevations instead of strong contrasts. Furthermore, I'll replace the aggressive gold glows with refined gold accents, such as borders.


**Optimizing Spacing and Style**

I'm now focusing on whitespace and CSS adjustments. Specifically, I'm updating the fonts to 'Outfit' and 'Lora' in the index file, and softening the blue gradient. Text shadow intensity is also being dialed back. In App.tsx, I'm cleaning up Tailwind classes, especially the strong shadows, and ensuring readability isn't compromised with lighter effects.

payload.text: quero que a seção principal tenha mais destaque, o site parece muito opaco

**Considering Hero Design**

I'm focusing on the Hero section now. My goal is to increase the contrast and visual impact there. The user feels the site is too opaque, so I'm aiming for vibrancy. Reducing the overall "opaqueness" is a top priority in my thinking.


**Optimizing Visual Punch**

I'm presently revising the Hero section's background and gradients in `index.html`. My focus is to create a more dynamic background, possibly with a subtle glow or clearer image. I plan to increase headline size and weight in `App.tsx` and improve the "card-glass" style. I'm also exploring using gold accents and ensuring text readability. I'm aiming for a "premium" feel.


**Refining Visual Focus**

I'm now zeroing in on visual hierarchy. I'm prioritizing the title's prominence and ensuring the call to action is distinct. The site needs a clearer flow, and I believe these modifications will make the page less "foggy".

**Designing the Menu Header**

I'm currently focused on designing a sticky, glassmorphic header menu for "Pacote Bíblia em Foco – Materiais Visuais." The goal is to make it visually appealing and user-friendly, maintaining its fixed position at the top of the webpage. I'm exploring different blur effects to achieve the glassmorphism aesthetic.


**Integrating Menu Functionality**

I'm now integrating the menu components and anchor links. I'm focusing on ensuring the semi-transparent background provides a clear view, even on different content. The "Offer Name" will stay prominent, and navigation links anchor to the correct sections with proper `id` attributes. The CTA will be strategically placed, of course. I'm also thinking how to maintain this on smaller screens and mobile devices.


**Optimizing Scroll Behavior**

I'm making sure to optimize the scroll behavior. While the browser's default smooth scrolling handles most things, I'll add a "scroll to top" feature if necessary. I'm also ensuring the fixed header doesn't obscure the content, adding padding to the main wrapper in `index.html` to address this issue.

payload.text: a seçao principal e a segunda, estao com um intervalo muito grande de uma para outra, diminua

**Adjusting the Layout**

I'm currently focused on the spacing. I've pinpointed the `pt-32 pb-24 md:pt-56 md:pb-6` padding in `App.tsx` that's creating the gap between the Hero and the "Por que a maioria trava" sections. My next step will be to tweak these values to reduce that interval.


**Narrowing the Spacing**

I've been working on refining the spacing between the Hero and the second section. The original padding between the two was quite excessive, adding up to 24rem on desktop. My adjustments involve decreasing the bottom padding of the Hero section, and adjusting the top padding of the second section. I'll be sure to preserve the background glow effects to ensure a smooth visual transition. Finally, I plan to assess other areas of the layout for similar opportunities to reduce whitespace.

payload.text: mantenha as cores mas faça um pouco mais claro apenas

**Retaining Original Colors**

I'm now revisiting the original color scheme, aiming for a lighter execution of the landing page, as requested. The white/light theme initially proposed is not quite hitting the mark, so I'm focusing on incorporating the dark blue, gold/amber, and parchment in a more subtle and airy manner. I am exploring how to accomplish this.


**Adjusting the Color Palette**

I'm now zeroing in on lighter executions for the backgrounds. The goal is a delicate dance between maintaining the brand's identity and creating the airy feeling requested. Specifically, I plan to brighten the `index.html` background to include more white space. The backgrounds of the `App.tsx` sections will use translucent whites or lighter slates to avoid the heavy "night mode" vibe. I'm focusing on text color contrast to ensure the gold still pops, and working on a "frosted" glassmorphism effect for the cards. I'm also planning to brighten the Hero Section.


**Finding the Balance**

I'm now zeroing in on striking the right balance. It seems the user wants me to find a middle ground between the heavy dark theme and the almost all-white version. I'm considering a lighter "dark mode," with more translucent containers and a light slate background, or a "light mode" that prominently features the brand's deep blue, as well. I'll need to figure out which approach is better.



**Optimizing UI Visibility**

I'm now shifting gears toward an improved dark UI, as the user may be seeking increased visibility rather than a complete light theme. I plan to brighten the slate background subtly by mixing a lighter shade, apply more inner glow and backlight to cards, and enhance the vibrancy of gold elements, and to reduce the intensity of the parchment texture to eliminate a "dirty" appearance. I think this will achieve better clarity.

I'm making progress updating the `testimonial` objects in `App.tsx` to include an `avatarUrl` property. I've chosen some high-quality placeholder images from Unsplash, aiming for generated photo styles that fit the personas: pastor, teacher, and student. The focus is to replace the letter-based icon with these avatars, while keeping the UI clean, clear, and premium. The plan is to ensure the images are rounded to match the design.


**Integrating Image URLs**

I've begun modifying the `App.tsx` file to display the `avatarUrl` property within the testimonials. As planned, the image is now the primary element, with the letter-based icon acting as a fallback. This update should ensure a visually engaging design, while keeping the UI clean.

Okay, I'm currently assessing the hero section. I've noted the current text alignment: left on desktop, centered on mobile. The headline is "O Estudo Bíblico com Clareza Máxima." I've been honing the hero section copy to maximize impact. I'm focusing on "benefit-driven" headlines like "Domine a Bíblia..." or "A Chave Visual...". The subheadline is now "Diga adeus à confusão..." and aims for more compelling language. I'm also ensuring the text container is centered on all screen sizes, mirroring a VSL style, with the CTA block centered too, and I'm refining the bullets for a more result-oriented approach. Finally, the button copy should be "QUERO MEU ACESSO IMEDIATO!"


**Implementing Visual Centering**

I'm now implementing the visual changes, specifically centering the hero section content. I'm focusing on layout adjustments and grid modifications to achieve this. Currently, I'm working in `App.tsx` and updating the hero container classes (using flex-col, items-center, and text-center) while ensuring the button and price are stacked vertically and centered. I also updated the text with the newest copy suggestions.


**Optimizing Visual Appeal**

I'm now fully committed to a premium landing page aesthetic, and I've started fully centering everything in the hero section for maximum impact, including headline, subheadline, bullets, price, and the button. I'm focusing on "attractive" and concise copy to drive engagement and ensure the design feels high-end.

**Analyzing Hero Components**

I'm currently focused on the hero section's elements. My main task is evaluating the removal of the CTA and price display from this crucial area. I'm examining how this change impacts user engagement and conversion. Furthermore, I'm thinking about the headline and subheadline elements.


**Clarifying Design Directions**

I'm now grappling with the user's conflicting requests regarding the hero section's CTA. Initially, they wanted a centralized CTA, but now explicitly request its removal. This inconsistency has prompted me to analyze possible interpretations of this directive, considering if it's a relocation to a sticky or bottom section. I am re-examining the `App.tsx` file for a clearer solution.


**Refining Hero Structure**

I'm now implementing the directive to remove the central price and button block from the hero section. My current focus is on maintaining a visually appealing and impactful hero area. I plan to refine the headline and subheadline to ensure a compelling value proposition. I am confident that these changes will deliver a cleaner, more focused hero section, and ensure that the user can still find a button soon after the hero section. I will check that the sticky button is still in place. I will update `App.tsx` and isolate the section I will remove.

**Eliminating Warranty Mentions**

I'm currently focused on removing all mentions of "Garantia." First, I'm working on the Hero section to eliminate the "Garantia 7 dias" badges. Then, I'll completely remove the Warranty section entirely, as specified in the original prompts. This ensures the app is streamlined and free of the term "Garantia" as requested.


**Removing Remaining References**

I've made progress on removing the remaining "Garantia" references. Specifically, I've identified and will eliminate the "7 Dias Garantia Incondicional" block within the "Oferta Final" section of `App.tsx` (around line 380) and the related FAQ entry on refunds (around line 405). My next step is to carefully scan for any other potential mentions. I'm focusing on ensuring a complete removal.


**Streamlining Text Elements**

I'm now identifying instances of "ou" and "7 dias" to remove them, streamlining the text for a cleaner presentation. I'm carefully reviewing all text elements to ensure complete removal of these phrases, which should promote a more concise and consistent user experience.

**Analyzing Button Copy**

I'm currently focused on the CTA text. I'm exploring options to improve it, aiming for something more engaging. The existing options are: "Garantir Acesso", "Quero meu acesso ao Pacote", "Quero acesso", etc., but I believe "Quero conhecer a Bíblia de verdade" would be a much better fit for the goal.


**Mapping Code Changes**

I've pinpointed the relevant buttons in `App.tsx`: header, mobile sidebar, "Oferta Final", and sticky footer. The plan is in place! I'll update "Garantir Acesso" to "Conhecer a Bíblia de Verdade", "Comprar Agora" to "Conhecer de Verdade", the other two as planned, and update any micro-copy.


**Polishing the Language**

I'm now refining the wording, striving for maximum impact within the design. The proposed copy is evolving: I've settled on "Conhecer a Bíblia" for the header, "QUERO CONHECER A BÍBLIA DE VERDADE" for the main offer button, and a simpler "QUERO CONHECER" for the sticky footer. I'm double-checking `App.tsx` for these elements.

Pare de apenas ler. Comece a
ENXERGAR as Escrituras. troque esta copy para outra chamativa tudo maisculo

**Revamping the Headline**

I'm currently working on updating the landing page headline. The goal is to replace the existing one with a much more impactful and eye-catching version, all in uppercase. I'm keeping the target audience in mind: pastors, preachers, EBD teachers, theology students, and Christian readers in general. I want it to grab attention!


**Finalizing the New Headline**

I've been iterating on the headline, moving toward something both punchy and aligned with the "Bíblia em Foco" brand. I've landed on "PARE DE APENAS LER. CHEGOU A HORA DE ENXERGAR A BÍBLIA DE VERDADE." The call to action is implicit now that the primary section doesn't have a button.


**Refining Headline Choices**

I'm now in a phase of refining the headline options for the landing page. I keep returning to the challenge of writing the headline in all caps. After several iterations, I've discarded some options for being either too long or too complex. I'm trying to balance directness with impact, keeping in mind the target audience. The current frontrunner is: "TRANSFORME SEU ESTUDO: DOMINE O CONTEXTO BÍBLICO COM CLAREZA VISUAL ABSOLUTA."


**Adjusting Headline Wording**

I've just made a few more tweaks to the headline. I've switched to "DOMINE O CONTEXTO BÍBLICO: ENXERGUE AS ESCRITURAS COM CLAREZA VISUAL ABSOLUTA." I've already updated the `h1` tag in `App.tsx`. I'm now also scanning the codebase for any other instances of "access" text that might need updating, based on previous user input and earlier requests to use "Quero conhecer a biblia de verdadae".

entries.description: Atualização da headline principal para uma versão mais impactante, em letras maiúsculas, mantendo o estilo premium e o foco em clareza visual.

payload.text: tire o neon do cta final

**Eliminating the Glow** I'm currently working to remove the neon effect from the final CTA. I've pinpointed the `gold-glow` class as the culprit in the `App.tsx` file. Now, I'm figuring out the best way to modify the CSS to achieve a more subtle visual for that section. I'm focusing on alternatives to maintain the section's impact without the excessive brightness. **Pinpointing the Culprit** I've homed in on the `gold-glow` class within the "Final Offer" section of `App.tsx`. Specifically, it's the price element (`37,90`) that's sporting the unwanted neon effect. I'm taking the steps to eliminate that. I'm also double-checking the code to ensure I haven't missed any other subtle "neon-like" effects in the final CTA area. The button itself seems fine. **Confirming Specificity Focus** I've determined that "final" likely refers to the "Final Offer" section. Thus, I'll restrict the removal of the `gold-glow` class to the price element within that section of `App.tsx`. I verified the code snippet: `37,90`. I am going to remove `gold-glow` from this, and this specific, occurrence. **Verifying Visual Impact** I'm now fully focused on removing the `gold-glow` class from the specified price element. I've confirmed this is the primary source of the neon effect. The button's `box-shadow` is subtle enough to leave be, and the headline is fine. I'm satisfied that removing `gold-glow` from the price in the final offer is the main change. The sticky mobile CTA price doesn't use the glow. The result will be a cleaner price, without the "neon" effect. **Considering Scope Extension** I am now assessing whether to extend the removal of the `gold-glow` class beyond the final offer. The hero section also features this class. While I'm inclined to stick to the explicit "cta final" instruction, the user's perception of "neon" may encompass all instances. But I think I will stick to what's defined. I've re-examined the code snippet in the final offer, confirming its prominence. I'll also ensure nothing else is 'neon' in the index.html. Ultimately, I am still only removing the class from that specific element for now.

entries.description: Remoção da classe 'gold-glow' (efeito neon) do preço na seção de oferta final para um visual mais limpo e sóbrio.

**Focusing the Frontend**

I'm now zeroing in on the frontend, specifically a landing page. I've got React code using Tailwind CSS in mind, the goal is to get this implemented. I'm taking the headline size in the Hero section into account for an enhanced user experience and clarity. I'll get this refined and in place!
